forecast-period.text: A high pressure system over the west and north of SA, will move steadily eastwards to be centred over western NSW by Saturday evening and over the Tasman Sea by Monday. Northerly winds freshen over the state on Tuesday ahead of a weakening cold front mid-week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Isolated fog patches about the agricultural area. Light showers about the southern agricultural area, contracting to southern coasts during the afternoon and evening. Maximum temperatures above average in the south and west, grading to below average in the northeast. Northwest to southwesterly winds about the agricultural area, light and variable in the northeast. Winds shifting northerly in the west.
Showers about the southern agricultural area, mainly about windward coasts and ranges. west to southwesterly winds about the agricultural area, light and variable in the north and west.

Isolated fog patches about central and eastern districts. Mainly dry, apart from isolated light showers about southern coasts. Maximum temperatures above average in the south and west, grading to below average in the northeast. Light winds in the east at first tending northeast to northerly from the west during the day.
Mostly dry. Maximum temperatures generally above average. Northeast to northwesterly winds, freshening in the west.

text.p: Dry apart from isolated showers developing about the far west and the northeast. Maximum temperatures above average. Freshening north to northwesterly winds, shifting west to southwesterly in the far southwest in the evening.
Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Tuesday: Generally less than 5 mm about the southern agricultural area, in the northeast and the southwest. Isolated falls of 2 to 10 mm possible.
